title: Traffic jams without bottlenecks—experimental evidence for the physical mechanism of the formation of a jam
authors: Sugiyama Y., Fukui M., Kikuchi M., Hasebe K., Nakayama A., Nishinari K., Tadaki S., Yukawa S.
notes: Canonical experimental demonstration of a phantom jam on a 230 m circular track. 22 drivers, target 30 km/h; bunching formed spontaneously and propagated backward. This is the existence proof that the instability is driver-intrinsic, not infrastructure-intrinsic.

title: Phase transition in traffic jam experiment on a circuit
authors: Tadaki S., Kikuchi M., Fukui M., Nakayama A., Nishinari K., Shibata A., Sugiyama Y., Yamada T., Yukawa S.
notes: Follow-up to Sugiyama 2008 with more participants and larger tracks; confirms the free→jammed transition is a dynamical phase transition and estimates critical density on a ring.

title: Quantitative explanation of circuit experiments and real traffic using the optimal velocity model
authors: Nakayama A., Fukui M., Kikuchi M., Hasebe K., Nishinari K., Sugiyama Y., Tadaki S., Yukawa S.
notes: Reconciles the Bando OV model with the Sugiyama/Tadaki ring data; estimates V(gap) from measured trajectories.

title: Dissipation of stop-and-go waves via control of autonomous vehicles: Field experiments
authors: Stern R.E., Cui S., Delle Monache M.L., Bhadani R., Bunting M., Churchill M., Hamilton N., Haulcy R., Pohlmann H., Wu F., Piccoli B., Seibold B., Sprinkle J., Work D.B.
notes: First AV stabilization demo on a Sugiyama-style ring road: a single controlled vehicle among 21 human drivers eliminated stop-and-go waves, reduced fuel ~40%, increased throughput ~15%. This is the foundational mixed-autonomy field result.

title: Stabilizing traffic flow via a single autonomous vehicle: Possibilities and limitations
authors: Cui S., Seibold B., Stern R.E., Work D.B.
notes: Introduces FollowerStopper and PI-with-saturation controllers. Proves stabilizability conditions for a single controlled vehicle on a ring under OV-type human drivers.

title: Flow: A Modular Learning Framework for Mixed Autonomy Traffic
authors: Wu C., Kreidieh A.R., Parvate K., Vinitsky E., Bayen A.M.
notes: Published Flow paper. Framework couples SUMO/Aimsun with RL. Reports up to 40% improvement with 5-10% AV penetration on ring and bottleneck benchmarks.

title: Flow: A Modular Learning Framework for Mixed Autonomy Traffic (arXiv preprint)
authors: Wu C., Kreidieh A.R., Parvate K., Vinitsky E., Bayen A.M.
notes: Earlier arXiv version of the Flow paper; first large-scale description of the framework.

title: Benchmarks for reinforcement learning in mixed-autonomy traffic
authors: Vinitsky E., Kreidieh A., Le Flem L., Kheterpal N., Jang K., Wu C., Wu F., Liaw R., Liang E., Bayen A.M.
venue: Proceedings of the 2nd Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL) PMLR 87:399-409
notes: Four mixed-autonomy benchmarks: ring, figure-8, merge, bottleneck. Standard comparison surface for RL traffic controllers.

title: Dissipating stop-and-go waves in closed and open networks via deep reinforcement learning
authors: Kreidieh A.R., Wu C., Bayen A.M.
notes: Reports near-complete wave dissipation at 10% CAV penetration in open networks; measurable suppression at 2.5%. Key evidence for low minimum penetration rate.

title: Emergent behaviors in mixed-autonomy traffic
authors: Wu C., Kreidieh A., Vinitsky E., Bayen A.M.
venue: Proceedings of the 1st Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL) PMLR 78:398-407
notes: A single RL-trained AV on a Sugiyama ring eliminates waves and learns to use a smaller safe distance than humans, exceeding theoretical equilibrium velocity.

title: Flow: Architecture and Benchmarking for Reinforcement Learning in Traffic Control
authors: Wu C., Kreidieh A., Vinitsky E., Bayen A.M.
notes: Originating arXiv draft laying out the benchmark ring-road and merge tasks used throughout the Berkeley mixed-autonomy line.

title: The Lord of the Ring Road: A Review and Evaluation of Autonomous Control Policies for Traffic in a Ring Road
authors: Chou F.-C., Bagabaldo A.R., Bayen A.M.
venue: ACM Transactions on Cyber-Physical Systems 6(1) Art.8
notes: Systematic benchmark of 10 AV controllers (FollowerStopper, PI-saturated, RL, LQR, etc.) on a ring at varying penetration and distribution. Finds RL policies are most consistently effective.

title: Designing, simulating, and performing the 100-AV field test for the CIRCLES consortium: Methodology and Implementation of the Largest mobile traffic control experiment to date
authors: Lee J.W., Wang H., Jang K., Lichtle N., Hayat A., Bunting M., Bhadani R., Nice M., Gumm M., Delle Monache M.L., Seibold B., Piccoli B., Sprinkle J., Work D.B., Bayen A.M.
notes: Methodology and execution paper for the 100-AV I-24 field test (MegaVanderTest, November 2022). Discusses data pipeline, controller selection, validation workflow, and operational lessons.

title: I-24 MOTION: An instrument for freeway traffic science
authors: Gloudemans D., Wang Y., Ji J., Zachar G., Barbour W., Hall E., Cebelak M., Smith L., Work D.B.
notes: Canonical description of the I-24 MOTION testbed: 276 cameras covering ~4.2 miles of I-24 near Nashville, producing trajectories of every vehicle at 10 Hz. Essential data infrastructure for the MegaVanderTest.

title: Virtual trajectories for I-24 MOTION: data and tools
authors: Gloudemans D., Barbour W., Gloudemans N., Cebelak M., Work D.B.
notes: Derives virtual Eulerian/Lagrangian trajectory products from the raw I-24 MOTION INCEPTION dataset. Useful format for downstream ML.

title: Are commercially implemented adaptive cruise control systems string stable?
authors: Gunter G., Gloudemans D., Stern R.E., McQuade S., Bhadani R., Bunting M., Delle Monache M.L., Lysecky R., Seibold B., Sprinkle J., Piccoli B., Work D.B.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems 22(11):6992-7003
notes: Seven 2018-model-year commercial ACC systems tested in platoon experiments. ALL were string-unstable; a 6 mph disturbance grew to 25 mph along a platoon. Pivotal finding: stock ACC is part of the problem, not the solution.

title: Congested traffic states in empirical observations and microscopic simulations
authors: Treiber M., Hennecke A., Helbing D.
notes: The Intelligent Driver Model (IDM). Foundational time-continuous CFM used in SUMO, Flow, and essentially every HDR simulator for phantom jams.

title: Dynamical model of traffic congestion and numerical simulation
authors: Bando M., Hasebe K., Nakayama A., Shibata A., Sugiyama Y.
notes: The Optimal Velocity (OV) model with V(gap) = tanh-like function. Linear stability analysis of ring roads gives the classical instability condition f'(h*)/(2a) > 1/2.

title: A behavioural car-following model for computer simulation
authors: Gipps P.G.
notes: Gipps collision-avoidance CFM: each driver sets a desired braking and acceleration limit. Default CFM in SUMO prior to IDM adoption.

title: Simulation des Strassenverkehrsflusses
authors: Wiedemann R.
venue: Schriftenreihe des Instituts für Verkehrswesen Universität Karlsruhe
notes: Psychophysical CFM with perceptual thresholds. Basis of the VISSIM (Wiedemann-99) model used throughout industry.

title: A simplified car-following theory: a lower order model
authors: Newell G.F.
notes: Newell's parsimonious car-following rule: follower trajectory is a space-time shift of leader's. Foundation of the triangular fundamental diagram in Lagrangian form.

title: Resurrection of 'Second Order' Models of Traffic Flow
authors: Aw A., Rascle M.
notes: Introduces the Aw-Rascle hyperbolic 2-eq macro model; replaces space derivative with convective derivative. Fixes the anisotropy defect of Payne-Whitham.

title: A non-equilibrium traffic model devoid of gas-like behavior
authors: Zhang H.M.
notes: Companion to Aw-Rascle, jointly forming the ARZ model. Standard macroscopic backbone for jamiton theory.

title: On kinematic waves II. A theory of traffic flow on long crowded roads
authors: Lighthill M.J., Whitham G.B.
venue: Proceedings of the Royal Society A 229(1178):317-345
notes: The LWR model. q=Q(ρ) conservation law; shock and rarefaction propagation theory. Foundation of first-order macroscopic traffic.

title: Shock Waves on the Highway
authors: Richards P.I.
notes: Independent derivation of the LWR first-order theory; shows queue-forming shocks and threshold density behavior.

title: Self-sustained nonlinear waves in traffic flow
authors: Flynn M.R., Kasimov A.R., Nave J.-C., Rosales R.R., Seibold B.
notes: Coins 'jamitons'. Shows ARZ-type models admit detonation-wave-analog solutions that match phantom jam observations, including backward propagation speed.

title: Constructing set-valued fundamental diagrams from jamiton solutions in second order traffic models
authors: Seibold B., Flynn M.R., Kasimov A.R., Rosales R.R.
notes: Shows jamiton-populated flow produces a set-valued (scattered) fundamental diagram matching empirical loop-detector scatter.

title: Jamitons: Phantom Traffic Jams
authors: Seibold B.
notes: Didactic exposition of jamitons for physics teachers; useful reference for intuition.

title: Traffic Flow Dynamics: Data Models and Simulation
authors: Treiber M., Kesting A.
notes: The primary reference textbook. Covers IDM, OV, Gipps, stability analysis, fundamental diagrams, and macroscopic models with worked examples. Source of default IDM parameters.

title: Traffic and related self-driven many-particle systems
authors: Helbing D.
notes: The standard review from the physics side. Covers microscopic, gas-kinetic mesoscopic, and macroscopic models; stability; and empirical observations.

title: Fundamentals of Transportation and Traffic Operations
authors: Daganzo C.F.
notes: Berkeley textbook treating kinematic waves, fundamental diagrams, and traffic operations rigorously; origin of the triangular fundamental diagram framing.

title: Vehicle Dynamics and Control (2nd ed)
authors: Rajamani R.
notes: Reference for low-level longitudinal control: cruise control, ACC, brake/throttle dynamics, time delays. Core for implementing the actuation side of the HDR loop.

title: Stochastic Transport in Complex Systems: From Molecules to Vehicles
authors: Schadschneider A., Chowdhury D., Nishinari K.
notes: Complementary statistical-physics textbook covering CA models (Nagel-Schreckenberg), mean-field theories, and non-equilibrium transport formalisms.

title: The Physics of Traffic: Empirical Freeway Pattern Features Engineering Applications and Theory
authors: Kerner B.S.
notes: Develops three-phase traffic theory (free flow, synchronized flow, wide moving jam). Alternative framing to second-order continuum theory.

title: Introduction to Modern Traffic Flow Theory and Control: The Long Road to Three-Phase Traffic Theory
authors: Kerner B.S.
notes: Extended Kerner treatment with engineering applications. Useful contrast to the ARZ/jamiton lineage.

title: String stability of interconnected systems
authors: Swaroop D., Hedrick J.K.
notes: Formal definition of string stability for linear unidirectional platoons. Core reference for what 'no amplification along a chain' means mathematically.

title: Car-following models: fifty years of linear stability analysis – a mathematical perspective
authors: Wilson R.E., Ward J.A.
notes: Cleans up the distinction between uniform-flow stability, platoon stability, and string stability. Gives general CFM framework and stability tests.

title: $\mathcal{L}_2$ and $\mathcal{L}_\infty$ stability analysis of heterogeneous traffic with application to parameter optimisation for the control of automated vehicles
authors: Monteil J., Bouroche M., Leith D.J.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology 27(3):934-949
notes: Gives L2 and L∞ string stability conditions for heterogeneous mixed human-AV flows. Provides LMI-based parameter tuning for AV controllers in mixed traffic.

title: On L∞ string stability of nonlinear bidirectional asymmetric heterogeneous platoon systems
authors: Studli S., Seron M.M., Middleton R.H.
notes: Extends L∞ string stability to nonlinear bidirectional platoons; relates L∞ bound to maximum overshoot.

title: Modeling cooperative and autonomous adaptive cruise control dynamic responses using experimental data
authors: Milanés V., Shladover S.E.
notes: Experimental CACC string stability demonstration using 4 vehicles on a PATH test track. CACC achieves first-order stable response; ACC is lightly damped second-order. Historical baseline.

title: Impacts of Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control on Freeway Traffic Flow
authors: Shladover S.E., Su D., Lu X.-Y.
notes: Microscopic simulation of CACC penetration sweeps. Shows CACC increases capacity meaningfully only at moderate-to-high penetration; ACC alone ~zero effect on capacity.

title: Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control: Definitions and Operating Concepts
authors: Shladover S.E., Nowakowski C., Lu X.-Y., Ferlis R.
notes: Defines the SAE J3016-related concepts of ACC vs. CACC and tests driver-selected time gaps with ACC enabled (most drivers choose 1.1 s or larger).

title: Effects of Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control on traffic flow stability
authors: Schakel W.J., van Arem B., Netten B.D.
notes: Early macroscopic simulation study suggesting CACC can damp shockwaves at 50% penetration using IDM-based controllers.

title: The Impact of Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control on Traffic-Flow Characteristics
authors: van Arem B., van Driel C.J.G., Visser R.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems 7(4):429-436
notes: Early microscopic simulation of CACC in a merge scenario using MIXIC; showed stability benefits above ~40% penetration.

title: Enhanced Intelligent Driver Model to access the impact of driving strategies on traffic capacity
authors: Kesting A., Treiber M., Helbing D.
venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A 368:4585-4605
notes: Introduces the 'IDM+' / ACC-IDM variant designed for simulation of adaptive cruise control vehicles under cut-ins. Sweeps ACC penetration vs. bottleneck capacity.

title: Jam-avoiding adaptive cruise control (ACC) and its impact on traffic dynamics
authors: Kesting A., Treiber M., Schönhof M., Kranke F., Helbing D.
notes: Designs a jam-avoiding ACC policy and shows macroscopic capacity benefits; precursor to the Kesting 2010 paper.

title: General Lagrangian approximation to one-dimensional physical traffic flow models
authors: Leclercq L., Laval J.A., Chevallier E.
notes: Explicit derivation of the Newell-type Lagrangian discretization of LWR; useful bridge between micro and macro views.

title: A mechanism to describe the formation and propagation of stop-and-go waves in congested freeway traffic
authors: Laval J.A., Leclercq L.
venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A 368:4519-4541
notes: Proposes that stop-and-go oscillations on multilane freeways are seeded by timid/aggressive driver heterogeneity during lane changes, rather than pure ring-road instability.

title: Freeway traffic oscillations: Microscopic analysis of formations and propagations using Wavelet Transform
authors: Zheng Z., Ahn S., Chen D., Laval J.
notes: NGSIM trajectory analysis using wavelets to identify oscillation formation and propagation. Good empirical reference for wave speeds and lifetimes.

title: Shock wave propagation in traffic flow: a microscopic analysis of the Sugiyama experiment
authors: Orosz G., Wilson R.E., Stépán G.
venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A 368:4455-4479
notes: Delay-differential-equation analysis of ring-road car-following with reaction time; Hopf bifurcation gives jamiton-like solutions.

title: Bifurcations and multiple traffic jams in a car-following model with reaction-time delay
authors: Orosz G., Wilson R.E., Krauskopf B.
notes: Foundational DDE analysis of OV model with delay; shows subcritical Hopf that produces multiple coexisting jam states.

title: Dynamics of connected vehicle systems with delayed acceleration feedback
authors: Ge J.I., Orosz G.
notes: Design of connected cruise control with V2V acceleration feedback; derives string-stability regions as a function of delay.

title: Connected cruise control: modelling delay effects and nonlinear behaviour
authors: Orosz G.
notes: Unified treatment of connected cruise control including delay and nonlinearity; comparison to ACC and CACC.

title: Effect of adaptive cruise control systems on mixed traffic flow near an on-ramp
authors: Davis L.C.
notes: Shows that moderate ACC penetration can raise bottleneck throughput by suppressing the upstream jam propagation.

title: Effect of ACC systems on traffic flow
authors: Davis L.C.
notes: Parametric study of ACC headway policies and their impact on flow instability in OV-type microscopic simulations.

title: Microscopic Traffic Simulation using SUMO
authors: Lopez P.A., Behrisch M., Bieker-Walz L., Erdmann J., Flötteröd Y.-P., Hilbrich R., Lücken L., Rummel J., Wagner P., Wießner E.
notes: Canonical SUMO 'launch paper' for version 1.0. Describes architecture, TraCI, IDM/Krauss CFMs, Wiedemann-99, and intermodal support.

title: SUMO - Simulation of Urban MObility: An Overview
authors: Krajzewicz D., Erdmann J., Behrisch M., Bieker L.
venue: SIMUL 2011 Third International Conference on Advances in System Simulation
notes: Earlier overview paper. Cites TraCI API, DLR context, and the basic Krauss car-following model that preceded IDM defaults.

title: Recent Development and Applications of SUMO
authors: Behrisch M., Bieker L., Erdmann J., Krajzewicz D.
venue: International Journal on Advances in Systems and Measurements 5(3&4):128-138
notes: Details on SUMO's lane-change model (LC2013), junction logic, and its then-new TraCI interface.

title: The highD Dataset: A Drone Dataset of Naturalistic Vehicle Trajectories on German Highways for Validation of Highly Automated Driving Systems
authors: Krajewski R., Bock J., Kloeker L., Eckstein L.
notes: ~110k vehicles, 45k km, 16.5 h, six locations, 420 m segments. Drone-captured; cleaner than NGSIM. Standard highway naturalistic trajectory resource.

title: Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) Vehicle Trajectories and Supporting Data
authors: Federal Highway Administration
venue: FHWA Data Portal (US-101 June 2005 I-80 April 2005)
notes: Canonical legacy dataset: US-101 and I-80 trajectories from video at 10 Hz. Known to have occasional measurement noise but remains the most cited highway trajectory dataset.

title: Estimation of desired time gaps parameter for IDM and corresponding speed-density relations
authors: Punzo V., Simonelli F.
notes: NGSIM calibration of IDM. Gives empirical ranges for the desired time gap T and maximum acceleration a.

title: Analysis and Model of Freeway Traffic Data
authors: Chen C., Skabardonis A., Varaiya P.
notes: PeMS-based analysis of freeway traffic, including bottleneck identification and flow-density scatter structure.

title: Systematic Identification of Freeway Bottlenecks
authors: Chen C., Skabardonis A., Varaiya P.
notes: Algorithm for automatically finding active freeway bottlenecks from loop-detector data. Not directly about phantom jams but needed for separating infrastructure vs. intrinsic jams.

title: Bivariate relations in nearly stationary highway traffic
authors: Cassidy M.J.
notes: Uses cumulative counts to show that under stationary conditions freeway data collapse to a well-defined 1D curve. Essential validation of the fundamental diagram concept at the empirical level.

title: Discussion of traffic stream measurements and definitions
authors: Edie L.C.
venue: Proc. 2nd International Symposium on the Theory of Traffic Flow pp.139-154
notes: Introduces Edie's generalized definitions of density and flow over a space-time region. Used in nearly every HDR evaluation function for macro quantities.

title: A Study in Highway Capacity
authors: Greenshields B.D.
notes: The original linear speed-density model q = ρ v_f (1 - ρ/ρ_jam). Pedagogical default.

title: The Cell Transmission Model: A Dynamic Representation of Highway Traffic Consistent with the Hydrodynamic Theory
authors: Daganzo C.F.
notes: CTM: discrete Godunov scheme for LWR with triangular fundamental diagram. Canonical cell-based macroscopic solver.

title: The Cell Transmission Model Part II: Network Traffic
authors: Daganzo C.F.
notes: CTM extension to networks with merges and diverges.

title: A Finite Difference Approximation of the Kinematic Wave Model of Traffic Flow
authors: Daganzo C.F.
notes: Theoretical justification of CTM as a Godunov scheme; convergence properties.

title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
authors: Schulman J., Wolski F., Dhariwal P., Radford A., Klimov O.
notes: PPO: the most-used on-policy algorithm for Flow/CIRCLES benchmarks.

title: Soft Actor-Critic: Off-Policy Maximum Entropy Deep Reinforcement Learning with a Stochastic Actor
authors: Haarnoja T., Zhou A., Abbeel P., Levine S.
notes: SAC: off-policy continuous-control algorithm used in recent mixed-autonomy RL.

title: Addressing Function Approximation Error in Actor-Critic Methods
authors: Fujimoto S., van Hoof H., Meger D.
notes: TD3: twin-Q off-policy baseline algorithm used in some traffic-control RL benchmarks.

title: Trust Region Policy Optimization
authors: Schulman J., Levine S., Moritz P., Jordan M., Abbeel P.
notes: TRPO: predecessor to PPO; used in early Flow experiments.

title: Continuous control with deep reinforcement learning
authors: Lillicrap T.P., Hunt J.J., Pritzel A., Heess N., Erez T., Tassa Y., Silver D., Wierstra D.
notes: DDPG. Original actor-critic for continuous control; sometimes used as a baseline for AV speed control.

title: Human-level control through deep reinforcement learning
authors: Mnih V. et al.
notes: DQN. Foundational deep-RL reference used when classifying actions rather than velocities.

title: A multiscale model for traffic regulation via autonomous vehicles
authors: Garavello M., Goatin P., Liard T., Piccoli B.
notes: Coupled PDE-ODE framework where AVs act as moving flux constraints in LWR. Basis for proving wave damping at the macroscopic level.

title: Scalar conservation laws with moving constraints arising in traffic flow modeling: an existence result
authors: Delle Monache M.L., Goatin P.
notes: First existence proof for LWR with a moving bottleneck constraint from an AV. Mathematical foundation of macroscopic AV control.

title: A PDE-ODE model for traffic control with autonomous vehicles
authors: Delle Monache M.L., Liard T., Piccoli B., Stern R.E., Work D.B.
notes: Extends the PDE-ODE framework to multiple AVs and gives numerical schemes. Direct bridge between the Stern field experiments and the continuum picture.

title: Multiscale control of generic second order traffic models by driver-assist vehicles
authors: Chiarello F.A., Piccoli B., Tosin A.
notes: Uses second-order macroscopic models with an AV density term to quantify the minimum AV fraction needed to stabilize a given equilibrium.

title: Nonlinear Optimal Control of Traffic Flow with Stability Guarantees
authors: Hayat A., Piccoli B., Truong S.
notes: Derives a Lyapunov-based AV controller with provable closed-loop stability guarantees for the ARZ model.

title: How many autonomous vehicles are required to stabilize traffic flow?
authors: Bahavarnia M., Taha A.F.
notes: Optimizes the number of AVs needed to stabilize a given human flow subject to control saturation. Frames the minimum-penetration question as a constrained optimization.

title: Taming Spontaneous Stop-and-Go Traffic Waves: A Computational Mechanism Design Perspective
authors: (anonymized author set; see arXiv record)
notes: Mechanism-design framing of the wave-suppression problem. Treats CAV control policy as the product of a throughput/oscillation tradeoff game; searches for the optimal policy as a function of density.

title: A Review of Stop-and-Go Traffic Wave Suppression Strategies: Variable Speed Limit vs. Jam-Absorption Driving
authors: He Z., Laval J., Han Y., Hegyi A., Nishi R., Wu C.
notes: Direct 2025 review of the two dominant suppression paradigms. Useful for contextualizing the HDR question: which of VSL-style eulerian or JAD-style lagrangian wins and under what penetration.

title: Stabilizing traffic flow by autonomous vehicles: Stability analysis and implementation considerations
authors: Yao Z., Wu Y., Wang Y., Zhao B., Jiang Y.
notes: Recent CFM-based stabilization analysis with practical notes on sampling rate, delay, and acceleration saturation.

title: Data-Driven Traffic Reconstruction and Kernel Methods for Identifying Stop-and-Go Congestion
authors: Zhao E., Wang H., Hayat A., Delle Monache M.L., Work D.B., Bayen A.M.
notes: Kernel-based stop-and-go wave identification from I-24 MOTION trajectories; provides a ground-truth labeling pipeline for HDR evaluation.

title: Modeling Monitoring and Controlling Road Traffic Using Vehicles to Sense and Act
authors: Delle Monache M.L., Sprinkle J., Vasudevan R., Work D.B.
venue: Annual Review of Control Robotics and Autonomous Systems 6:149-174
notes: Recent CIRCLES-authored survey of mobile-actuator traffic control including sensing trade-offs and the field-test methodology.

title: Achieving string stability of ACC vehicles via feedback on acceleration and jerk
authors: Santhanakrishnan K., Rajamani R.
notes: Jerk-feedback controller design that provably achieves string stability for ACC platoons.

title: String stability of automated vehicle platoons operating in the absence of V2V communications
authors: Al Alam A., Gattami A., Johansson K.H.
notes: Early study showing that without V2V feedback, time-gap policies must exceed ~1.4 s for ACC to achieve string stability.

title: Optimal Control Theoretic View on Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control
authors: Wang M., Daamen W., Hoogendoorn S.P., van Arem B.
notes: MPC-based CACC that treats the CACC problem as a distributed optimal control problem.

title: Rolling horizon control framework for driver assistance systems. Part II: Cooperative sensing and cooperative control
authors: Wang M., Daamen W., Hoogendoorn S.P., van Arem B.
notes: Companion paper to [89] with implementation-level MPC details.

title: Model Predictive Control for Distributed Systems
authors: Camponogara E., Jia D., Krogh B.H., Talukdar S.
notes: General reference for distributed MPC; relevant to platoon-level control formulations.

title: Receding horizon network control
authors: Jia D., Krogh B.H.
notes: Early networked MPC reference used as theoretical background for distributed CACC MPCs.

title: Model predictive control system design and implementation for vehicle platooning
authors: Dunbar W.B., Caveney D.S.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems 13(3):1145-1158
notes: Distributed MPC for platoon control with formal stability certificates.

title: Longitudinal vehicle controller design for IVHS systems
authors: Hedrick J.K., McMahon D.H., Narendran V.K., Swaroop D.
notes: Classical PATH-era platoon longitudinal control reference.

title: Automatic Highway Systems - A Final Report
authors: Shladover S.E.
notes: PATH program summary; historical AHS demonstrations precursor to modern mixed autonomy.

title: Automated Vehicle Control Developments in the PATH Program
authors: Varaiya P.
notes: Seminal IVHS / automated highway systems architecture paper. Establishes the platoon/string control paradigm.

title: Smart cars on smart roads: problems of control
authors: Varaiya P.
notes: Classical control-theoretic view of automated highway systems.

title: The Karlsruhe Lane Change Assistant: A first result of the KONVOI project
authors: Konvoi project group
venue: 5th European Congress and Exhibition on Intelligent Transport Systems
notes: KONVOI CACC truck platoon field demonstration (Germany). Early real-world CACC field data.

title: Experimental investigation of cooperative adaptive cruise control on tracks
authors: Milanés V., Shladover S.E., Spring J., Nowakowski C., Kawazoe H., Nakamura M.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems 15(1):296-305
notes: PATH/Nissan four-vehicle CACC field experiments on a closed track; string stability demonstrated experimentally with a 0.6-0.9 s time gap.

title: Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control Implementation in the Grand Cooperative Driving Challenge
authors: Kianfar R., Augusto B., Ebadighajari A., Hakeem U., Nilsson J., Raza A., Tabar R.S., Irukulapati N.V., Englund C., Falcone P., Papanastasiou S., Svensson L., Wymeersch H.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems 13(3):994-1007
notes: Details one of the GCDC-2011 winning CACC implementations.

title: Decentralized cooperative lane-changing decision-making for connected autonomous vehicles
authors: Wang M., Hoogendoorn S.P., Daamen W., van Arem B., Happee R.
notes: Cooperative lane-change MPC in mixed autonomy; matters for practical wave suppression when lane changes seed oscillations.

title: Simulation to scaled city: zero-shot policy transfer for traffic control via autonomous vehicles
authors: Jang K., Vinitsky E., Chalaki B., Remer B., Beaver L., Malikopoulos A.A., Bayen A.M.
venue: International Conference on Cyber-Physical Systems (ICCPS) pp.291-300
notes: Zero-shot RL-to-real transfer on the University of Delaware scaled city. Closes part of the sim-to-real gap for traffic RL.

title: Quantifying air quality benefits resulting from few autonomous vehicles stabilizing traffic
authors: Stern R.E., Chen Y., Churchill M., Wu F., Delle Monache M.L., Piccoli B., Seibold B., Sprinkle J., Work D.B.
notes: Air-quality gains from the Stern 2018 ring field test: NOx -4% to -10%, fuel -40%.

title: An overview of vehicular platoon control under the four-component framework
authors: Zheng Y., Li S.E., Li K., Borrelli F., Hedrick J.K.
notes: Review article classifying platoon controllers by node dynamics, information flow topology, formation geometry, and control law.

title: Stability and Scalability of Homogeneous Vehicular Platoon: Study on the Influence of Information Flow Topologies
authors: Zheng Y., Li S.E., Wang J., Cao D., Li K.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems 17(1):14-26
notes: Proves how predecessor-only, predecessor-leader, and bidirectional topologies affect scalability and string stability.

title: Cooperative driving: an ant colony system for autonomous intersection management
authors: Dresner K., Stone P.
notes: AI-style intersection management for CAVs. Relevant as downstream destination for wave-free freeway flow.

title: Traffic shockwave attenuation via variable speed limits
authors: Hegyi A., De Schutter B., Hellendoorn H.
notes: Model-predictive VSL controller that smooths shockwaves. Eulerian counterpart to Lagrangian AV control.

title: Dynamic traffic control using SPECIALIST: a Variable Speed Limit algorithm for shock-wave suppression
authors: Hegyi A., Hoogendoorn S.P., Schreuder M., Stoelhorst H., Viti F.
notes: Field trial of the SPECIALIST VSL algorithm on a Dutch motorway; significant reduction in wide moving jams.

title: Model predictive control for variable speed limit systems
